# BitPolar

> Near-optimal vector quantization library implementing Google's TurboQuant (ICLR 2026), PolarQuant (AISTATS 2026), and QJL (AAAI 2025). Compresses embeddings to 3-8 bits with provably unbiased inner products and zero training. 58 integrations across every major AI framework.

BitPolar is the canonical open-source implementation of data-oblivious vector quantization. It compresses high-dimensional vectors (embeddings, KV cache entries) using polar coordinate encoding with 1-bit Johnson-Lindenstrauss residual correction. No training data, no codebooks, no calibration — the quantizer is fully defined by 4 integers: (dimension, bits, projections, seed).

## Key Properties

- Data-oblivious: no training, no codebooks, no calibration data
- Deterministic: fully defined by (dim, bits, projections, seed)
- Provably unbiased: E[estimated_inner_product] = exact_inner_product
- Near-optimal: distortion within 2.7x of Shannon rate-distortion limit
- Instant indexing: 600x faster than Product Quantization

## Install

- Rust: `cargo add bitpolar`
- Python: `pip install bitpolar`
- JavaScript (WASM): `npm install bitpolar-wasm`
- Node.js: `npm install bitpolar-native`
- Go: `go get github.com/mmgehlot/bitpolar/bitpolar-go`
- Java: Maven Central `io.github.mmgehlot:bitpolar:0.3.3`
- PostgreSQL: `cargo pgrx install`
- Docker: `docker pull ghcr.io/mmgehlot/bitpolar-server`

## Quick Start (Python)

```python
import numpy as np
import bitpolar

q = bitpolar.TurboQuantizer(dim=384, bits=4, projections=96, seed=42)
embedding = np.random.randn(384).astype(np.float32)
code = q.encode(embedding)
score = q.inner_product(code, embedding)
```

## Quick Start (Rust)

```rust
use bitpolar::TurboQuantizer;
use bitpolar::traits::VectorQuantizer;

let q = TurboQuantizer::new(384, 4, 96, 42).unwrap();
let code = q.encode(&vector).unwrap();
let score = q.inner_product_estimate(&code, &query).unwrap();
```

## 58 Integrations

RAG Frameworks: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Haystack, DSPy, FAISS (drop-in replacement), ChromaDB
Agentic AI: LangGraph, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK, Google ADK, Anthropic MCP, AutoGen, SmolAgents, PydanticAI, Agno
Agent Memory: Mem0, Zep, Letta (MemGPT)
Vector Databases: Qdrant, Milvus, Weaviate, Pinecone, Redis, Elasticsearch, PostgreSQL, DuckDB, SQLite, Supabase, Neon
LLM Inference: vLLM, HuggingFace Transformers, llama.cpp, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, Ollama, ONNX Runtime, Apple MLX
ML Frameworks: PyTorch (torchao), JAX/Flax, TensorFlow/Keras, scikit-learn
Cloud: Spring AI (Java), Vercel AI SDK, AWS Bedrock, NVIDIA Triton

## References

- TurboQuant (ICLR 2026): https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.19874
- PolarQuant (AISTATS 2026): https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.02617
- QJL (AAAI 2025): https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.03482
